verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the cytoplasmic face of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation (inner-leaflet anchored). Its kinase domain faces the cytoplasm. However, tumor cells are well-documented to display SRC (and active pY416-SRC) on the outer leaflet as a cancer ecto-kinase. This cell-state-induced ecto-SRC pool on the outer leaflet of transformed/cancer cells qualifies as contextual surface accessibility under the cell_state_induced bucket. Baseline localization is inner-leaflet/cytoplasmic, so 'yes' is not appropriate.
